Resolve Server Ops work. Apply when: connecting to or verifying Resolve, choosing compound vs granular (--full) tools, opening/closing the local control panel, reading MCP resources, or diagnosing environment/dependency problems.

Two servers — compute offline, apply live:
- Live (running Resolve, `davinci-resolve`): setup (schema/get_defaults/set_defaults/clear_defaults), resolve_control (launch/get_version/get_page/open_page, control panel lifecycle, api_truth/verification_stats/env_audit), timeline_versioning (begin_run/list_versions/diff_versions/rollback)
- Offline (.drp/.drt/.drx + DB, no Resolve, `davinci-resolve-advanced`): none — server-ops is live-server infrastructure with no advanced-server counterpart
- Key rule: Call resolve_control(action="launch") first in a new session — connects to or starts Resolve, safe to call when already running. timeline_versioning is the snapshot/rollback layer other domains rely on, not a content-editing tool itself. scripts/doctor.py and the advanced capabilities tool diagnose environment/dependency gaps.

Depth: docs/kernels/server-ops-kernel.md. On-demand in any MCP client: the `/server_ops_workflow` slash prompt. Claude Code skill: `resolve-server-ops`. Never modify/transcode/derive source media (AGENTS.md).
